repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Papers documenting Maynard's career at Cornell, including his involvement in establishing the School of Nutrition.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Professor of Biochemistry and Nutrition; director of the School of Nutition,1941-1955; special consultant to the U.S. Interdepartmental Committee for Nutrition and National Defense.

After receiving his Ph.D. in chemistry from Cornell University in 1915 Leonard Amby Maynard was appointed assistant professor in the College of Agriculture there. In 1934 he served as visiting professor at the University of Nanking, China, and assisted in a survey of the nutrition of the Chinese farm family. At Cornell he directed the Animal Nutrition Laboratory and the U.S. Plant, Soil, and Nutrition Laboratory. He was named commissioner in charge of nutrition for the New York State Emergency Food Commission during World War II., and late in 1947 was appointed to a new food commission established by Governor Thomas E. Dewey. He wrote ANIMAL NUTRITION (1937); coauthored BETTER DAIRY FARMING with Elmer S. Savage; and wrote numerous publications on biochemistry and nutrition.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Included are administrative papers pertaining to four executive positions held by Professor Maynard (1940-49): director of the U.S. Plant, Soil, and Nutrition Laboratory, New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences (1940-45); director of the School of Nutrition, Cornell University (1941-49); head of the Department of Biochemistry and Nutrition, N.Y.S. College of Agriculture and Life Sciences (1945-49); and head of the Laboratory of Animal Nutrition, N.Y.S. College of Agriculture and Life Sciences (1940-49). Includes correspondence dealing with the establishment (1941) of the School of Nutrition and efforts to obtain financial support for it, administrative matters, nutrition research, the London Nutrition Conference (1951) and other professional meetings, Maynard's lecture invitations, and his evaluation of scientific manuscripts sent him; inquiries from individuals and institutions, with Maynard's replies, chiefly concerning the nutritive value of milk and enriched bread and of butter as opposed to margarine; and notes (June 1941) from discussions with President Edmund Ezra Day on the establishment of the School and notes (1951, 1952) relating to its present and future programs. Correspondents include Sanford S. Atwood, Howard E. Babcock, Cornelis W. de Kiewiet, Edward R. Eastman, Willard I. Emerson, Anson W. Gibson, David Birney Hand, Herman E. Hilleboe, Asa S. Knowles, Deane W. Malott, William I. Myers, Weyland Pfeiffer, and Fred Sexauer.

Also, an interview (47-2-O.H. 31, 114 page typescript) conducted in 1963 in six sessions by Gould P. Colman, in which Maynard discusses his graduate work, the establishment of a chemical laboratory in the Department of Animal Husbandry, research and graduate instruction, nutrition studies at other institutions in the 1920s, work on open formula feeds and other studies done with the GLF (Cooperative Grange League Federation Exchange, Inc., now Agway), the administration of the U.S. Plant, Soil, and Nutrition Laboratory at Cornell, the development and administration of the Department of Biochemistry and the School of Nutrition, nutrition practices and study in China in 1934, England during World War II., Germany at the end of the war, and Central America and elsewhere following his retirement, the work of ICA and FAO (Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations), and his association with H. E. Babcock, J. Lossing Buck, Lewis Knudson, Albert R. Mann, and Elmer Seth Savage.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Leonard A. Maynard papers, #29-1-292. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: School of Nutrition files: Research reports done for Consolidation Edison Co.
Correspondence of Laboratory of Animal Nutrition, A-Z
Biochemistry and nutrition correspondence, files A-W
Leonard A. Maynard's manuscripts for publication; correspondence and publications of various organizations and subjects
Miscellaneous notes not Included in lectures
Letters of transmittal and replies - History of the School
Babcock Memorial Fund - DeGraff as Professor
Early Years of the Graduate School of Nutrition at Cornell, by Leonard A. Maynard
Maynard. Report of Chairman of Standing Committee on Nutrition, Ninth Pacific Science Congress
UK System vs. Atwater, S and D article, letters to Matt
History of Institute of Nutrition - Correspondence
Data from Stevens on cellulose metabolism
The Calorie Content of Urine of Human Beings and the Estimation of Metabolizable Energy of Foodstuffs
Energy-yielding components of food and computation of calorie values
Notes on calculation of energy values
EN discussion of the terms digestibility, availability and fuel value. W.O. Atwater Storrs Report, 1899
Available carbohydrates Atwater System of calculating the caloric value of diets
Guide to terminology in Biomedical Res.
Michigan studies of soil effect on milk SPN
Application Lilly Endowment, Inc; 813 Merch. Bk. Bld., Indianapolis 4, Ind. (weight and nutrition 1951)
118th American Association for the Advancement of Science meeting, Philadelphia and correspondence
E.R. Eastman (American Agriculturist) W.I. Emerson, Vice President, University Development
Foster, Edward S., General Secretary New York State Farm Bureau Federation, Ithaca. Federal Security Agency, Public Health Service, application for grant
National Council of Farmer Cooperatives, Chicago
Gibson, A.W., Director Resident Instruction, Roberts Hall
Guterman, C.E.F., Director Agricultural Experiment Station, Roberts Hall
Hoblitzelle Award, New York Regional Committee
Harrell Heath News- Albany. Nutritional broadsides
Dr. David B. Hand, Agricultural Experiment Station
Correspondence with President, Malott, D.W.; Act. Wright, T.P.; act. de Kiewiet, C.W.

"Early Years of the graduate School of Nutrition at Cornell, 1941-1956 ." Published by the Graduate School of Nutrition, Cornell University, in 1968, giving a history of the establishment of the School of Nutrition in 1941. 104 page pamphlet
Letter, Howard E. Babcock to James McConnell concerning the School of Nutrition
Partial reprint set of L.A. Maynard's articles
Transcript of interview with Maynard by Gould Colman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also listed in guide for collection #13-6-428

did.unittitle: Fifty-five page manuscript "Diary of Army Service" written by Maynard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accounting of Maynard's years as a member of American Expeditionay Forces in WWI, including photographs and telegram concerning his official commission.

did.unittitle: Text transcript of the diary made by husband of Maynard's granddaughter, Roger Ferlo

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All photographs are of Maynard in military uniform, including a wedding photo.

did.unittitle: Document appointing Maynard a First Lieutenant in the Chemical Warfare Service
Document appointing Maynard a Captain in the Chemical Warfare Service of the U.S. Army
Document appointing Maynard a Major in Chemical Warfare Service of the U.S. Army
Document appointing Maynard a Major in the Chemical Warfare Service of the Officers' Reserve Corp.
Two letters of commendation to Maynard from Chief of Chemical Warfare Services
Correspondence from Maynard to his father, Edward Maynard
Correspondence from Maynard to his sister, Harriet Maynard
Photograph of Maynard in China when he was a visiting professor
Letter to Maynard from Dwight Eisenhower

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter thanking Maynard for his service as a member of the Committee of the Arts and Sciences.

did.unittitle: Invitation to Eisenhower and Nixon inauguration
Honorary doctorate conferred by Wesleyan University
Honorary doctorate conferred by the University of Rhode Island
